title: Combination of three paprs
body: Very rough ATM, minimal cleanup done, but maybe could work?

:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #6) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Needs a rebase.

@rhatdan @mheon said there's no reason other than "sexiness" to do the build inside a container.  I'm not opposed to it in practice...except for...your way introduces a tight-coupling between the host-setup, and the testing.  IMHO, best to have a gap there, b/c it makes it easier to swap one host environment for another.  And/Or, having multiple VMs running the same testing steps (supported by papr).  I think @baude had in mind to do something like this

All that said, are you married to the container-way?  (I'm happy to amend this PR to only realize the division of labor part.)

It's not sexiness - you should *always* do builds in containers, and don't have that stuff on your host.  Even [for workstations](https://pagure.io/workstation-ostree-config).  There are many reasons (security), and you'll be a lot less likely to [hit package management disasters](https://twitter.com/KentonVarda/status/915620660755312640).  And further once you do this, it's *just as easy* to spin up e.g. CentOS/RHEL/whatever containers and use those.
@cgwalters or...we do it both ways :D
FWIW I think "Fedora Atomic" *is* Fedora too.  So I'd title it "Test in Fedora Cloud and Fedora Atomic".

> FWIW I think "Fedora Atomic" is Fedora too. So I'd title it "Test in Fedora Cloud and Fedora Atomic".

True true, an easy fix, thx.
I would prefer if the tests would happen outside of the container, since things like SELinux will not be tested if running inside of a container.
@rhatdan latest push does both, in and out of a container.  However @baude wants to take this over, so closing...

In [bubblewrap's papr](https://github.com/projectatomic/rpm-ostree/pull/513) we [build in a container, then install to the host and test there](https://github.com/projectatomic/bubblewrap/blob/master/ci/papr.sh#L11).
Note specifically the use of e.g. `ostree admin unlock` etc.

Another great example of this pattern that I mentioned earlier is projectatomic/atomic, which @baude is intimately familiar with :). Specifically, notice how [the PAPR YAML](https://github.com/projectatomic/atomic/blob/master/.papr.yml) simply calls out [the same script](https://github.com/projectatomic/atomic/blob/master/.papr.sh) for all three platforms.
@jlebon @cgwalters I think we're all in general agreement.  Thanks for the links.  @baude is taking this over.
